specs.section_path: 15-Layer Summary Table > 3.2 The Dynamic Spine: The Unified Phase Dynamics Equation (UPDE) > The Impedance-Matching Operator and Causal Horizon > Quasicriticality with MS-QEC: Two-Timescale Control and Certificates (revision 11.00) > Two-timescale structure (definitions).
source_formulae: P0R02959: Fast channel (stabilisers): MS-QEC and local feedback acting with gain G_f on timescale tau_f (tau_f tau_s). Its job is error suppression and coherence maintenance. Slow channel (exploration): controlled drift in the sigma1 band with gain G_s on timescale tau_s. Its job is to maintain the sensitivity, diversity, and manoeuvrability of phase relations. Separation assumption: tau_f/tau_s 1 (singular perturbation regime). Disturbances and modelling error are treated as bounded inputs.
P0R02961: [IMAGE:Ein Bild, das Text, Screenshot, Diagramm, Schrift enthlt. KI-generierte Inhalte knnen fehlerhaft sein.]

P0R02962: Fig.: Two-Timescale Control & BIBO Certificate. Fast MS-QEC stabilisers (gain GfG_fGf, tauf\tau_ftauf) and slow exploration (GsG_sGs, taus\tau_staus), tauf/taus1\tau_f/\tau_s\ll1tauf/taus1. Scheduling via affective sensitivity: Gf=Gf,min+kfA/sigma+kfsigma1G_f=G_{f,\min}+k_f|\partial A/\partial\sigma|+k_f'|\sigma-1|Gf=Gf,min+kfA/sigma+kfsigma1, Gs=Gs,max window(sigma1) [1tanh(cA/sigma)]G_s=G_{s,\max}\,\mathrm{window}(|\sigma-1|\le\delta)\,[1-\tanh(c|\partial A/\partial\sigma|)]Gs=Gs,maxwindow(sigma1)[1tanh(cA/sigma)]. Composite Lyapunov: Vtotal=Vfast+Vslow+ crossV_{\text{total}}=V_{\text{fast}}+V_{\text{slow}}+\varepsilon\,\text{cross}Vtotal=Vfast+Vslow+cross -> VtotalfVfastsVslow+input2 \dot V_{\text{total}}\le -\alpha_f V_{\text{fast}}-\alpha_s V_{\text{slow}}+\kappa\|{\rm input}\|^2VtotalfVfastsVslow+input2 (BIBO). Torus inset: fast contracts transverse error; slow drives neutral cycles.

specs.section_path: 15-Layer Summary Table > 3.2 The Dynamic Spine: The Unified Phase Dynamics Equation (UPDE) > The Impedance-Matching Operator and Causal Horizon > Quasicriticality with MS-QEC: Two-Timescale Control and Certificates (revision 11.00) > Two-timescale structure (definitions).
source_formulae: P0R02963: Gain scheduling via the Affective Field.

P0R02964: Let sigma be the layer-appropriate criticality coordinate (distance-to-criticality proxy). We schedule gains using the local sensitivity of affective drive to criticality, A/sigma: Raise G_f when |A/sigma| is large (affective steepness risk), and when |sigma1| grows (drifting off the band). Raise G_s when |sigma1| is small and |A/sigma| is modest (flat affective landscape near sigma1 safe exploration).

P0R02965: Operational law (one admissible family): G_f(sigma) = G_f,min + k_f |A/sigma| + k_f |sigma1|, G_s(sigma) = G_s,max window(|sigma1| ) [1 tanh(c |A/sigma|)],with setting the quasicritical band and {k_f, k_f, c} tuned per layer. The Affective Field definition and sigma-geometry are as per the Self/Affective sections.
